description_long: This is a large, rustic log cabin constructed primarily from thick, rounded timber logs with a dark, shingled gable roof. The structure features multiple intersecting rooflines, prominent overhangs, and exposed wooden beams, giving it a robust and traditional mountain lodge appearance. Stonework is visible at the base and around the chimney, adding to the sturdy, natural aesthetic. Several windows of varying sizes are distributed asymmetrically across the facade, framed by wood and grouped in clusters, with a prominent balcony above the main entrance. The front of the cabin includes a covered porch area with wooden railings, while the rear and sides show additional architectural details such as a secondary chimney and more window groupings. The overall design suggests a spacious, multi-room interior suitable for residential use in a forested or mountainous urban neighborhood.
description: A large, rustic log cabin with a dark gable roof, stone base, and multiple asymmetrical windows, featuring a covered porch and prominent wooden beams.
description_view_0: The main facade shows the covered porch, balcony, central entrance, and a mix of stone and timber construction with clustered windows.
description_view_1: A direct view of the front highlights the porch, balcony, and symmetrical roof overhangs, with visible wooden railings.
description_view_2: The side view reveals the stone chimney, additional windows, and the log construction extending along the length of the cabin.
description_view_3: The rear view displays intersecting rooflines, a secondary chimney, and a less prominent entrance or porch area.
attribute_roof_type: multi-gabled roof with intersecting gable sections, deep overhangs, and dark shingle cladding
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple small to medium rectangular windows, asymmetrically distributed in clusters and singles across all visible facades, with a prominent group above the main entrance and additional windows on the sides
attribute_door_placement: one main exterior door centered on the front facade beneath the balcony, with possible secondary entrances on the sides or rear not clearly visible
